title: Fix Admin Panel routing for hash-based SPA navigation

requirements.text: Fix the Admin Panel routing in App.tsx so it is accessible via the hash-based URL pattern used by the deployed site (e.g. /#/admin or /#admin). The current routing logic must be updated to detect the /admin path within the URL hash (window.location.hash) rather than relying on window.location.pathname, since the deployed ICP canister serves the SPA via hash routing. The admin panel at https://jigarchaudhary-tyf.caffeine.xyz/#caffeineAdminToken=... must remain accessible, and navigating to /#/admin (or the equivalent hash-based path) must render the AdminPanel component.

quotes: this is my url for website, website is working fentastic but admin pannel is not working
Admin panel at /admin not accessible on deployed site — site uses hash-based routing, admin route needs to be /#/admin or similar hash-based path

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to https://jigarchaudhary-tyf.caffeine.xyz/#/admin renders the AdminPanel component instead of the main site
The main public site continues to load correctly at the root URL
The existing hash-change and popstate event listeners in App.tsx are updated to check for the /admin segment within the hash
The AdminPanel is able to fetch and display booking submissions from the backend getAllSubmissions() method once the routing is fixed
A direct link to /#/admin opens the admin panel without a blank screen or redirect to the home page

constraints: Do not change the backend Motoko actor or any backend logic
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Preserve the existing caffeineAdminToken hash parameter passthrough — it must not interfere with admin panel detection
nonGoals: Adding authentication or login protection to the admin panel
Changing the admin panel UI or table columns
Migrating from hash-based routing to a full React Router DOM path-based setup
Any changes to the public-facing sections of the site
